evidences.content: The adaptation of Colleen Hoover’s Regretting You continues to get into the plus column for Paramount. Despite being the fourth-worst reviewed film of the year to open in over 3,000 theaters (28%) ahead of only Flight Risk (27%), Smurfs (21%), and Love Hurts (19%), it made another $1.5 million this weekend to bring its 31-day total to $47.2 million. Approaching $90 million globally, the $30 million production is in the black for Paramount and the most profitable film on their 2025 theatrical slate. [...] Fresh at 94% on the Tomatometer. Currently, the sequel is Fresh at 96%. [...] Hikari, who has directed episodes of Tokyo Vice for HBO MAX and Beef for Netflix, released their English-language feature debut with Rental Family, starring Brendan Fraser. The film has earned a strong reception from critics since its festival debuts at Toronto and elsewhere. It’s only Searchlight Pictures’ second release of 2025 after The Roses was dumped at the end of August and made $15.2 million (still their fourth-highest grosser since the pandemic). evidences.title: For Good' is wicked good at the box-office, conjures record-setting ...
evidences.content: ### Original story, Nov. 21 There’s no place like the multiplex this weekend as Wicked: For Good flies into theaters with a projected $150 million–$180 million domestic debut. With global totals expected to soar past $200 million, the sequel is on track to deliver the biggest opening of 2025 — and rewrite the record books for Broadway adaptations on the big screen. [...] Last year’s Wicked opened to $112.5 million domestic and $164 million worldwide, setting the best start ever for a movie based on a stage musical. For Good is primed to break that benchmark with ease, thanks to strong presales, franchise recognition, and heightened interest in the final chapter of Elphaba (Cynthia Erivo) and Glinda’s (Ariana Grande) saga. Early reviews, however, are more mixed this time: the film sits at 71 percent on Rotten Tomatoes, below the original’s 88 percent score with critics. [...] Universal is tempering expectations, projecting a $125 million+, but industry modeling continues to climb heading into the weekend. Anything north of $162 million would dethrone A Minecraft Movie for 2025’s highest opening. Thursday previews brought in an estimated $20 million-plus, which would put the seque...

evidences.title: Box Office: 'Wicked For Good' Opens to Record-Smashing $147M in ...
evidences.content: The female-fueled movie shattered numerous opening records everywhere. Domestically, it boasts the biggest start ever for a Broadway musical adaptation after besting last year’s Wicked ($112.5 million) and the third-biggest of all time among any musical behind 2019’s The Lion Kingand 2017’s Beauty and the Beast, not adjusted for inflation. It is also the third-biggest North American opening ever for a Universal title behind Jurassic World ($208.8 million) and Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom ($148 million); the second-best for the weekend before Thanksgiving behind The Hunger Games: Catching Fire ($158.1 million); and the second-best three-day launch of 2025 to date behind A Minecraft Movie ($208.9 million). Lilo & Stitch, opening over Memorial Day, collected $147 million for the three-day [...] over Memorial Day, collected $147 million for the three-day weekend and $182.6 million for the four-day holiday frame. [...] Chu’s film likewise passed up Wicked overseas to rank as the top launch ever for a Broadway adaptation. It is also the fifth-biggest opening of all time for any musical behind The Lion King (2019), Moana 2, Frozen 2 and Beauty and the Beast. Lastly, it passed up Supe...

evidences.title: Box Office: 'Wicked: For Good' Opening To $150M, 'A' CinemaScore
evidences.content: Wicked: For Good arrives as a much-needed boost for the post-Labor Day frame box office, which through Monday was behind 2.2% from the same period a year ago per Comscore with $1.29 billion (and down 31% compared to the post-Labor Day to November 18 period of 2019). While the pre-Thanksgiving weekend last year with Wicked minted north of $202 million for all movies, that frame also had the R-rated, male-skewing Gladiator II, which debuted to $55M. Even without a Gladiator II-like movie in the mix this weekend, some believe Wicked: For Good can make up the difference in its potential overperformance. Most holdovers in the top 10 a year ago dropped 50% or steeper. We’ll see what can survive, but again, the upcoming holiday week will make up for any deficiencies this weekend. [...] NRG tracking showed $130M on the low end for Wicked: For Good heading into the weekend, but the pre-sales indicated that the movie would surpass that projection. [...] best advance ticket sales of any PG movie ever). Also, the Grande, Erivo, Idina Menzel and Kristin Chenoweth performances went viral.

[...] 3rd Highest Opening Weekend for a Movie Musical Of All-Time At The Domestic Box Office, behind 2019’s The Lion King ($191.7M) and 2017’s Beauty and the Beast ($174.7M) and ahead of Moana 2 ($139.7M Friday-Sunday take in what was a $225.4M five-day opening over Thanksgiving last year). 3rd Highest Opening Weekend at November Domestic Box Office after Black Panther: Wakanda Forever ($181.3M) and The Hunger Games: Catching Fire ($158M). 4th Biggest Global Hollywood Movie Opening of 2025 behind Jurassic World R...

evidences.title: Wicked: For Good Brings in $226 Million at the Global Box Office ...
evidences.content: The blockbuster musical sequel, which sees Ariana Grande and Cynthia Erivo reprise their roles as Glinda and Elphaba, secured $150 million domestically and $76 million internationally. Globally, Wicked: For Good is the fourth-best Hollywood debut of the year behind only the Lilo & Stitch live-action remake ($341 million), Jurassic World Rebirth ($322.5 million), and A Minecraft Movie ($313 million). It’s just ahead of the $220 million opening weekend James Gunn’s DC Universe film Superman brought in, and the $218 million MCU film The Fantastic Four made during the same period. [...] It also set a number of box office records. Wicked: For Good secured the biggest global opening for a Broadway musical adaptation, ahead of its predecessor, which brought in $164.2 million globally during opening weekend. Domestically, it’s the biggest debut for a Broadway musical adaptation, again ahead of last year’s Wicked ($112.5 million). And Wicked: For Good goes straight in at No. 5 in the biggest opening weekends for a musical of all-time, behind 2019’s The Lion King remake ($433 million), Moana 2 ($389 million), Frozen 2 ($358.4 million), and 2017’s Beauty and the Beast remake ($357 million).

evidences.title: 'Wicked: For Good' soars to record-breaking debut at box office
evidences.content: NBC Los Angeles # ‘Wicked: For Good' even more popular than the first, soars to record-breaking debut ## It's the biggest opening ever for a Broadway musical adaptation. #### By Lindsey Bahr | The Associated Press • Published November 23, 2025 • Updated on November 23, 2025 at 12:57 pm Access Hollywood spoke to Cynthia Erivo ahead of her hosting duties at the upcoming Tony Awards on Sunday. She teased what fans can expect with the upcoming film, “Wicked For Good.” Universal Pictures’ two-part “Wicked” gamble continues to defy gravity at the box office. [...] Two other films also opened in wide release this weekend, but further down on the charts behind a buffet of holdovers. Searchlight Pictures opened its Brendan Fraser film “Rental Family” in 1,925 theaters where it earned $3.3 million. The Finnish action film “Sisu: Road to Revenge," a Sony release, also played in 2,222 theaters. It earned an estimated $2.6 million. Second place went to “Now You See Me: Now You Don’t” with $9.1 million in its second weekend, followed by “Predator: Badlands” with $6.3 million in weekend three. “The Running Man” followed in fourth place with $5.8 million, down 65% from its debut last weekend. "Domestic" refers to gross box-office revenue from North America (US, Canada, and Puerto Rico) (IMDb - Box Office Mojo FAQ). Confused? Check our FAQ or ask us for help. evidences.title: Wicked: For Good Box Office Hits Major Milestone Immediately After ...
evidences.content: 2025's Wicked: For Good is only the fifth movie of the year to have earned more than $300 million in domestic theaters, behind Jurassic World Rebirth ($339.6 million), Superman ($354.2 million), Lilo & Stitch ($423.8 million), and A Minecraft Movie ($423.9 million). By hitting this milestone, the Wicked: For Good release has doubled its reported budget of $150 million at the domestic box office alone before the end of its third full week in theaters. Blockbuster movies typically need to earn back two and a half times their budgets in theaters in order to turn a profit, which could place For Good's estimated break-even point as high as $375 million. However, it has already surpassed that milestone worldwide with a cumulative global gross of $446.8 million. [...] Enjoy ScreenRant's box office coverage? evidences.title: HISTORY: A massive opening weekend just pushed 'Wicked
evidences.content: ## Boardroom's Post ### Boardroom November 24, 2025 · HISTORY: A massive opening weekend just pushed ‘Wicked: For Good’ into the record books.⁠ ⁠ The film opened with $150 million domestic and $226 million globally, topping the first movie and setting the biggest global and domestic opening ever for a Broadway adaptation. It also marked career-best openings for Ariana Grande, Cynthia Erivo, Jon M. Chu, Stephen Schwartz, and producer Marc Platt.⁠ ⁠ ‘For Good’ scored the biggest first day and preview totals for a Broadway musical adaptation and the biggest November opening for a movie musical. It also ranks as the year’s No. 2 domestic opening behind ‘A Minecraft Movie,’ Universal’s second-biggest domestic opening ever, and one of the top global debuts of 2025.⁠ ⁠

evidences.title: 'Wicked: For Good' soars to $223m at global box office ...
evidences.content: By Ben Dalton2025-11-24T08:50:00+00:00 'Wicked: For Good' 'Wicked: For Good' Source: Universal ‘Wicked: For Good’ Worldwide box office: November 21-23 [...] For Good recorded 35% of its global opening weekend from international markets, up from the 31% for Wicked. Among musical titles, the international start is also above those of Wonka, The Little Mermaid and Mamma Mia 2, and more than double that of Les Miserables. It is in line with 2025 summer blockbusters Mission: Impossible – The Final Reckoning and F1. Its $76m international result was boosted by a confirmed $24.7m in the UK and Ireland – further details to follow in the UK-Ireland box office report. It also soared in Australia with $8.6m, accounting for 78% of the top 10 takings and recording the biggest opening for a live-action musical, the biggest non-holiday opening of the year, Universal’s third-biggest opening of all time, and the third-biggest November opening ever. evidences.title: Wicked: For Good Worldwide Box Office - Surpasses An Iconic ...
evidences.content: Wicked: For Good Worldwide Box Office Update Wicked: For Good, directed by Jon M. Chu, is nearing the 50-day mark in theaters and is still making its presence felt at the global box office. With a current worldwide total of $518.6 million (till January 5, 2026, as last reported by Box Office Mojo), the musical fantasy sequel is now just $3.3 million short of overtaking its next 2025 target – The Fantastic Four: First Steps, which stands at $521.9 million worldwide. If audience turnout holds steady through the remainder of its theatrical run, surpassing that figure appears to be within reach.

evidences.title: Wicked: For Good Opens to $226 Million Globally, Fourth Best of Year
evidences.content: an image, when javascript is unavailable # ‘Wicked: For Good’ Flies to $223 Million Globally in Fourth-Biggest Box Office Debut of 2025 By Rebecca Rubin ### Rebecca Rubin Senior Film and Media Reporter #### Latest Cynthia Erivo is Elphaba in WICKED FOR GOOD, directed by Jon M. Chu. This weekend, the languishing box office has been changed for the better with “Wicked: For Good,” which powered to $223 million globally. Those ticket sales rank as the fourth-biggest worldwide debut of 2025, behind Disney’s “Lilo & Stitch” remake ($341 million), “Jurassic World Rebirth” ($322 million) and “A Minecraft Movie” ($313 million) and ahead of “Superman” ($220 million) and “The Fantastic Four” ($218 million). ## Related Stories DisneyPlus Hulu Bundle Promo [...] “Moviegoing is roaring back this weekend, and it will strengthen again with ‘Zootopia 2,'” says Gross, while noting that attendance remains seriously challenged. “These are tough times, with a few strong titles breaking through and even setting records.” #### Read More About: ## More from Variety Spotify - podcasts ### Spotify Paid Out a Record $11 Billion to the Music Industry in 2025 Olivia Dean performs onstage at the Spotify Best...
